questions.q: In which year was NITI Aayog established?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog was established on 1 January 2015 as the successor to the Planning Commission.

questions.q: What does the acronym NITI in NITI Aayog stand for?
questions.explanation: NITI stands for the National Institution for Transforming India.

questions.q: Which body did NITI Aayog replace?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog replaced the Planning Commission, which had functioned since 1950.

questions.q: Who serves as the ex-officio Chairperson of NITI Aayog?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister of India is the ex-officio Chairperson of NITI Aayog.

questions.q: NITI Aayog is best described as the government's premier:
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog serves as the government's premier policy think tank.

questions.q: One of the guiding principles of NITI Aayog is to foster which kind of relationship between the Centre and states?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog was created to foster cooperative federalism among the Centre and the states.

questions.q: In which city is NITI Aayog headquartered?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog is headquartered in New Delhi.

questions.q: NITI Aayog was created through which kind of instrument?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog was set up by a Union Cabinet resolution, making it a non-constitutional, non-statutory body.

questions.q: Unlike its predecessor, NITI Aayog does not have the direct power to allocate funds to states; this distinguishes it from the:
questions.explanation: Unlike the Planning Commission, NITI Aayog does not allocate plan funds; it functions primarily as an advisory think tank.

questions.q: The full strength of the NITI Aayog's apex council includes the Prime Minister and the heads of which governments?
questions.explanation: The Governing Council of NITI Aayog includes the Chief Ministers of states and Lieutenant Governors of union territories.

questions.q: The day-to-day functioning of NITI Aayog is overseen by which official?
questions.explanation: A Chief Executive Officer, appointed by the Prime Minister, manages the day-to-day functioning of NITI Aayog.

questions.q: The body within NITI Aayog comprising the Prime Minister and all Chief Ministers is called the:
questions.explanation: The Governing Council of NITI Aayog comprises the Prime Minister, Chief Ministers and Lieutenant Governors.

questions.q: Who was the first Vice-Chairperson of NITI Aayog?
questions.explanation: Economist Arvind Panagariya served as the first Vice-Chairperson of NITI Aayog.

questions.q: NITI Aayog publishes an index ranking states on which broad area of human well-being?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog publishes indices tracking states on areas such as health, education and water management.

questions.q: The NITI Aayog initiative aimed at rapidly improving the most underdeveloped districts is called the:
questions.explanation: The Aspirational Districts Programme, launched in 2018, is a NITI Aayog initiative to develop India's most backward districts.

questions.q: The flagship programme run by NITI Aayog to promote innovation and entrepreneurship is called the:
questions.explanation: The Atal Innovation Mission, including Atal Tinkering Labs, is run under NITI Aayog to foster innovation.

questions.q: NITI Aayog is structured around which two main hubs reflecting its dual focus?
options: Team India and Knowledge and Innovation Hub
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog is organised around the Team India Hub and the Knowledge and Innovation Hub.

questions.q: Members of NITI Aayog who are nominated experts but do not hold full-time positions are called:
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog includes part-time members drawn from leading universities, research organisations and relevant fields.

questions.q: What rank is generally held by the Vice-Chairperson of NITI Aayog?
questions.explanation: The Vice-Chairperson of NITI Aayog is accorded the rank of a Cabinet Minister.

questions.q: Instead of fixed Five-Year Plans, NITI Aayog has worked on longer documents such as a vision document spanning how many years?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog developed a long-term framework including a fifteen-year vision document, replacing the Five-Year Plan model.

questions.q: NITI Aayog serves primarily in which role with respect to the government?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog functions in an advisory capacity, providing policy inputs to the Centre and the states.

questions.q: Which secretariat-level coordinating mechanism between Centre and states does NITI Aayog use, that the Planning Commission lacked emphasis on?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog emphasises a bottom-up approach, involving states in policymaking, unlike the earlier top-down model.

questions.q: On which exact date did NITI Aayog formally come into existence?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog came into existence on 1 January 2015 through a Union Cabinet resolution.

questions.q: Who succeeded Arvind Panagariya as the Vice-Chairperson of NITI Aayog?
questions.explanation: Economist Rajiv Kumar succeeded Arvind Panagariya as Vice-Chairperson of NITI Aayog in 2017.

questions.q: Who served as the first Chief Executive Officer of NITI Aayog?
questions.explanation: Sindhushree Khullar was the first Chief Executive Officer of NITI Aayog.

questions.q: NITI Aayog's index measuring states' progress on global development goals tracks which framework?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog produces the SDG India Index tracking states' progress on the Sustainable Development Goals.

questions.q: Apart from the Governing Council, NITI Aayog can constitute which type of bodies to address specific issues across states?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog can form Regional Councils to address specific issues affecting more than one state or region.

questions.q: The first Health Index released by NITI Aayog ranked states using data primarily on which kind of indicators?
questions.explanation: The NITI Aayog Health Index ranked states on a composite of health outcomes, governance and process indicators.

questions.q: Which annual ranking by NITI Aayog assesses the ecosystem for new businesses across states?
questions.explanation: The India Innovation Index, published by NITI Aayog, ranks states on their innovation ecosystems.

questions.q: NITI Aayog's mandate explicitly excludes which function that the Planning Commission performed?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog does not allocate financial resources to states, a function the Planning Commission had performed.
